title_en: Marriage and Commerce in Tuhfat al-Muhtaj
content_en:
The marriage and commercial law chapters of Tuhfat al-Muhtaj represent Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's most extended contribution to Shafi'i private law, addressing the practical questions of Muslim communities across a vast geographic range. His fatwas on marriage and commercial questions were sought from Egypt to Southeast Asia, and Tuhfat al-Muhtaj incorporates the analytical framework that generated those responses.

Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's treatment of the invalid marriage contract and its consequences is particularly thorough. A marriage lacking a required condition (such as the wali's presence or the two witnesses) is invalid (fasid or batil in Shafi'i terminology). He addresses what happens to the couple who have lived as husband and wife in an invalid marriage: the marriage must be dissolved, any sexual relations that occurred while the invalidity was unknown are not treated as fornication (shubuha — error — prevents the application of had punishment), and the wife is entitled to mahr al-mithl as compensation for the intimacy that occurred.

The chapter on divorce presents the Shafi'i school's position on the triple talaq with its evidential basis. Three pronouncements of divorce — whether in one sitting or across three separate instances — produce the major irrevocable divorce (ba'in kubra). The Shafi'i school's position that three pronouncements in one sitting count as three is defended with the historical evidence that the Companions treated it as such until 'Umar ibn al-Khattab's decree, and that 'Umar's decree was a policy decision (siyasah) to deter hasty divorce rather than a reversal of the legal ruling.

Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's treatment of the mut'ah (gift of consolation) after divorce without specified mahr or before consummation reflects the Shafi'i position that it is obligatory (wajib) in some circumstances and recommended in others. The Quranic verse (2:236) establishing mut'ah as obligatory when divorce occurs before consummation and without a specified mahr is cited as the primary evidence.

In commercial law, Tuhfat al-Muhtaj addresses with particular depth the question of trading in items whose ultimate use may be prohibited. Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's strict position — that a seller who knows their product will be used for prohibited purposes is legally complicit in the prohibition — had practical consequences for commerce in his era and has continued relevance today. He extends this to the case of selling neutral items to someone who is known to be committed to their prohibited use, applying the principle that intent and knowledge affect legal character.

Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's chapter on gharar (excessive uncertainty) in commercial contracts provides the conceptual framework for evaluating novel contract types. He identifies the key criterion for impermissible gharar: the uncertainty must be: present in the subject matter itself (not merely about market conditions), significant enough to generate dispute and harm, and avoidable without defeating the contract's commercial purpose. This tripartite criterion provides a useful analytical tool for evaluating whether a novel commercial arrangement falls within the Islamic prohibition of gharar.
title_id: Pernikahan dan Perdagangan dalam Tuhfat al-Muhtaj
content_id:
Bab-bab hukum pernikahan dan perdagangan dari Tuhfat al-Muhtaj merupakan kontribusi Ibn Hajar al-Haytami yang paling luas terhadap hukum privat Syafi'i, membahas pertanyaan-pertanyaan praktis dari komunitas-komunitas Muslim di rentang geografis yang sangat luas. Fatwa-fatwanya tentang pertanyaan-pertanyaan pernikahan dan perdagangan diminta dari Mesir hingga Asia Tenggara, dan Tuhfat al-Muhtaj menggabungkan kerangka analitis yang menghasilkan respons-respons tersebut.

Perlakuan Ibn Hajar al-Haytami terhadap akad nikah yang tidak sah dan konsekuensinya sangat menyeluruh. Pernikahan yang kekurangan syarat yang diperlukan (seperti kehadiran wali atau dua saksi) adalah tidak sah (fasid atau batil dalam terminologi Syafi'i). Ia membahas apa yang terjadi pada pasangan yang telah hidup sebagai suami istri dalam pernikahan yang tidak sah: pernikahan harus dibubarkan, hubungan seksual apa pun yang terjadi saat ketidakabsahan tidak diketahui tidak diperlakukan sebagai zina (syubhah — kekeliruan — mencegah penerapan hukuman had), dan istri berhak atas mahr al-mitsl sebagai kompensasi atas keintiman yang terjadi.

Bab tentang talak menyajikan posisi mazhab Syafi'i tentang talak tiga beserta dasar dalilnya. Tiga ucapan talak — baik dalam satu majlis atau dalam tiga kesempatan terpisah — menghasilkan talak ba'in kubra (talak besar yang tidak dapat dirujuk). Posisi mazhab Syafi'i bahwa tiga ucapan dalam satu majlis dihitung sebagai tiga dipertahankan dengan bukti historis bahwa para Sahabat memperlakukannya demikian sampai keputusan 'Umar ibn al-Khaththab ra, dan bahwa keputusan 'Umar adalah keputusan kebijakan (siyasah) untuk mencegah talak yang tergesa-gesa daripada pembalikan hukum.

Perlakuan Ibn Hajar al-Haytami tentang mut'ah (hadiah penghiburan) setelah cerai tanpa mahr yang ditentukan atau sebelum persetubuhan mencerminkan posisi Syafi'i bahwa itu adalah wajib dalam beberapa keadaan dan dianjurkan dalam keadaan lainnya. Ayat Al-Qur'an (2:236) yang menetapkan mut'ah sebagai wajib ketika cerai terjadi sebelum persetubuhan dan tanpa mahr yang ditentukan dikutip sebagai bukti utama.

Dalam hukum perdagangan, Tuhfat al-Muhtaj membahas dengan kedalaman khusus pertanyaan berdagang dalam barang-barang yang penggunaan akhirnya mungkin dilarang. Posisi ketat Ibn Hajar al-Haytami — bahwa penjual yang mengetahui produknya akan digunakan untuk tujuan yang dilarang secara hukum terlibat dalam larangan tersebut — memiliki konsekuensi praktis bagi perdagangan di eranya dan tetap relevan sampai sekarang. Ia memperluas ini ke kasus menjual barang-barang netral kepada seseorang yang diketahui berkomitmen pada penggunaan yang dilarang tersebut, menerapkan prinsip bahwa niat dan pengetahuan mempengaruhi karakter hukum.

Bab Ibn Hajar al-Haytami tentang gharar (ketidakpastian berlebihan) dalam akad-akad perdagangan memberikan kerangka konseptual untuk mengevaluasi jenis-jenis kontrak baru. Ia mengidentifikasi kriteria kunci untuk gharar yang tidak diizinkan: ketidakpastian harus: ada dalam objek kontrak itu sendiri (bukan sekadar tentang kondisi pasar), cukup signifikan untuk menimbulkan sengketa dan kerugian, dan dapat dihindari tanpa menggagalkan tujuan komersial kontrak. Kriteria tripartit ini memberikan alat analitis yang berguna untuk mengevaluasi apakah suatu pengaturan komersial baru termasuk dalam larangan Islam tentang gharar.
content_ar: The marriage and commercial law الأبواب of Tuhfat al-Muhtaj represent Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's most extended contribution to الشافعي private law, addressing the practical questions of مسلم communities across a vast geographic range. His fatwas on marriage and commercial questions were sought from Egypt to Southeast Asia, and Tuhfat al-Muhtaj incorporates the analytical framework that generated those responses. Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's treatment of the invalid marriage contract and its consequences is particularly thorough. A marriage lacking a required condition (مثل the wali's presence or the two witnesses) is invalid (fasid or batil in الشافعي terminology). He addresses what happens to the couple who have lived as الزوج and الزوجة in an invalid marriage: the marriage must be dissolved, any sexual relations that occurred بينما the invalidity was unknown are not treated أماnication (shubuha — error — prevents the application of had العقاب), and the الزوجة is entitled to mahr al-mithl as compensation for the intimacy that occurred. The الباب on divorce presents the الشافعي school's position on the triple talaq with its evidential basis. Three pronouncements of divorce — whether in one sitting or across three separate instances — produce the major irrevocable divorce (ba'in kubra). The الشافعي school's position that three pronouncements in one sitting count as three is defended with the historical evidence that the الصحابة treated it as such حتى 'عمر ibn al-Khattab's decree, and that 'عمر's decree was a policy decision (siyasah) to deter hasty divorce rather than a reversal of the legal ruling. Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's treatment of the mut'ah (الهبة of consolation) بعد divorce without specified mahr or قبل consummation reflects the الشافعي position that يجب (الواجب) in some circumstances and recommended in others. The القرآني الآية (2:236) establishing mut'ah as obligatory عندما divorce occurs قبل consummation and without a specified mahr is cited as the الأساسي evidence. In commercial law, Tuhfat al-Muhtaj addresses with particular depth the question of trading in items whose ultimate use may be prohibited. Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's strict position — that a seller who knows their product will be used for prohibited purposes is legally complicit in the prohibition — had practical consequences for commerce in his era and has continued relevance today. He extends this to the case of selling neutral items to someone who is known to be committed to their prohibited use, applying the principle that intent and العلم affect legal الأخلاق. Ibn Hajar al-Haytami's الباب on gharar (excessive uncertainty) in commercial contracts provides the conceptual framework for evaluating novel contract types.
